meta.terminology_note: This test was run on 2026-04-13 when 'PLL' referred to 'Pipeline Lock List' (the 6 rules). On 2026-04-19, 'Pipeline Lock List' was retired and replaced by 'Compositional Integrity Protocol (CIP)'. PLL now means Phase-Locked Loop only. Q09 answers below reflect the pre-2026-04-19 terminology and were correct at time of test.
meta.commit_full: PENDING — pre-test-harness commit, test ran on local identical copy
meta.notes: Fresh agent with zero HUF context. Abbreviated test (5 of 10 questions). Repo access blocked by egress proxy — tested against local files identical to commit 26c2ba5.

Q01_naming.answer: Entropy-Invariant Time Transformer. NEVER: Ternary Transform, Temporal Transform, Time Transfer

Q03_japan.answer: 2013-2014. Fukushima March 2011 but annual data absorbs shock by 2013-2014. NOT 2011-2012.

Q05_governance.answer: Open-loop: HUF-GOV observes, human decides, loop stays open. Instrument reads, never actuates.

Q06_formula.answer: Flag when d_A(t→t+1) > μ+2σ. d_A(x,y) = ‖clr(x)−clr(y)‖. Thresholds: DE 5.5634, JP 5.6909, UK 2.8008

findings: Repo access blocked by Cowork egress proxy. Structural review deferred to ChatGPT run.

overall.verdict: FAST_REFRESH.json is an excellent cold-start briefing. All 5 tested questions answered with HIGH confidence from a single file read. File is canonical, precise, and well-organized. Missing: worked examples, explicit file-routing for next steps.
recommendations: Add next_steps field pointing to specific files for specific tasks
Add one worked example per formula showing input → output
failure_modes: repo_access_blocked — platform infrastructure, not repo issue
